educationalNotes.summary: Retinal layer receiving raw RGB pixel matrices.
educationalNotes.detailed: Accepts normalized three-channel RGB images of size 224x224. This spatial standardization ensures weight compatibility in subsequent fully connected layers.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Establishes spatial dimensions. Resizing inputs to 224x224 is required prior to forward propagation.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Fixed input sizes are critical to prevent spatial alignment errors in standard CNN dense layers.

calculationSteps.label: Kernel Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: 64 independent filters of size 7x7 operating over 3 input channels. No bias is used because it is immediately followed by Batch Normalization.

educationalNotes.detailed: Slides 64 different 7x7 kernels with a stride of 2 to extract general contours while shrinking spatial grid sizes.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Shrinks resolution from 224x224 to 112x112, reducing downstream computation.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Early pooling and large stride convolutions preserve core shapes while minimizing compute requirements.

calculationSteps.label: Trainable Parameters (gamma, beta)
calculationSteps.explanation: Scale parameter (gamma) and shift parameter (beta) for active channel scaling.

calculationSteps.label: Non-trainable Parameters (mean, variance)
calculationSteps.explanation: Running mean and running variance tracked during training to normalize inference inputs.

educationalNotes.detailed: Batch Normalization shifts activations to mean 0 and variance 1 across batch dimensions to avoid internal covariate shifts.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: BN layers are essential to stabilize gradient updates in deep CNNs.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: BN layers are essential to stabilize gradient updates in deep CNNs.

educationalNotes.detailed: Calculates f(x) = max(0, x) element-wise. Keeps active regions positive and sets negative features to zero.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: ReLU is computationally simple and prevents gradient saturation for positive inputs.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: ReLU allows learning complex non-linear combinations of edges.

educationalNotes.summary: Pads borders to control MaxPooling dimensions.
educationalNotes.detailed: Adds zero borders around the 112x112 feature map to expand it to 114x114, ensuring MaxPooling dimensions fit correctly.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Ensures pooling doesn't ignore borders.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Zero padding avoids border information loss.

educationalNotes.detailed: Extracts the maximum active pixel in overlapping 3x3 cells with stride 2.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Reduces downstream spatial dims and introduces local translation invariance.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: MaxPooling reduces resolution while filtering peak activations.

calculationSteps.label: Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: 1x1 bottleneck convolution to adjust dimensionality.

educationalNotes.detailed: This 1x1 filter reduces channels to 64 prior to the expensive 3x3 convolution, saving parameters.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Controls computation scale in deep bottleneck blocks.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: 1x1 convolutions act as spatial-preserving channel projection blocks.

calculationSteps.label: Trainable Parameters (gamma, beta)
calculationSteps.explanation: Scale parameter (gamma) and shift parameter (beta) for active channel scaling.

calculationSteps.label: Non-trainable Parameters (mean, variance)
calculationSteps.explanation: Running mean and running variance tracked during training to normalize inference inputs.

educationalNotes.detailed: Normalizes the compressed activation space before applying spatial convolutions.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Ensures inputs to downstream spatial convolutions are scaled identically.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Stabilizes inputs for subsequent spatial convs.

educationalNotes.detailed: Introduces activation non-linearity.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Allows complex feature mapping.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Introduces activation non-linearity.

calculationSteps.label: Kernel Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: 3x3 spatial convolution for feature extraction.

educationalNotes.detailed: Extracts patterns (corners, junctions) on the bottleneck channel representation. Stride of 1 handles spatial downsampling.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Combines spatial and channel features on the compressed bottleneck representation.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Performs spatial convolutions on the reduced volume to save weights.

calculationSteps.label: Trainable Parameters (gamma, beta)

calculationSteps.label: Non-trainable Parameters (mean, variance)
calculationSteps.explanation: Running mean and running variance tracked during training to normalize inference inputs.

educationalNotes.detailed: Scales features before the final dimension projection.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Restores normalization properties.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Maintains spatial scale consistency.

educationalNotes.detailed: Prepares signals for bottleneck expansion.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Locks in positive features.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Non-linear activation.

calculationSteps.label: Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: Restores dimensionality back to representation capacity.

educationalNotes.detailed: Expands output from 64 channels back to 256 channels, restoring visual descriptors.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Prepares the tensor size to match the original skip connection shape.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Expansion restores the high-dimensional capacity of features before residual adding.

calculationSteps.label: Non-trainable Parameters (mean, variance)

educationalNotes.detailed: Normalizes final features before additions.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Controls scales on residual additions.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Ensures stable signals for addition.

calculationSteps.label: Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: Projects shortcut channels.

educationalNotes.detailed: Ensures the shortcut tensor matches the main branch shape 256 for element-wise addition.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Required to project spatial and channel dimensions.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Used only when spatial grid or channels change between blocks.

calculationSteps.label: Trainable Parameters (gamma, beta)

calculationSteps.label: Non-trainable Parameters (mean, variance)
calculationSteps.explanation: Running mean and running variance tracked during training to normalize inference inputs.

educationalNotes.detailed: Applies Batch Normalization to the shortcut projection branch.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Maintains normal variance across shortcut outputs.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Normalizes the projected skip connection.

educationalNotes.summary: Performs element-wise residual addition: F(x) + x.
educationalNotes.detailed: Adds the output of the convolutional sandwich to the skip connection. This enables gradients to flow directly backwards.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: The core mathematical mechanism that avoids vanishing gradients in deep ResNets.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Identity loops guarantee stable gradient updates.

educationalNotes.detailed: Rectifies elements to introduce non-linearity at the block output.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Prepares activated features for the next block.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Final activation step of the bottleneck block.

calculationSteps.explanation: Scale parameter (gamma) and shift parameter (beta) for active channel scaling.

calculationSteps.explanation: Running mean and running variance tracked during training to normalize inference inputs.

educationalNotes.detailed: Scales features before the final dimension projection.

educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Maintains spatial scale consistency.

educationalNotes.detailed: Prepares signals for bottleneck expansion.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Locks in positive features.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Non-linear activation.

calculationSteps.label: Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: Restores dimensionality back to representation capacity.

educationalNotes.detailed: Expands output from 512 channels back to 2048 channels, restoring visual descriptors.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Prepares the tensor size to match the original skip connection shape.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Expansion restores the high-dimensional capacity of features before residual adding.

calculationSteps.label: Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: Projects shortcut channels.

educationalNotes.detailed: Ensures the shortcut tensor matches the main branch shape 2048 for element-wise addition.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Required to project spatial and channel dimensions.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Used only when spatial grid or channels change between blocks.

educationalNotes.detailed: Applies Batch Normalization to the shortcut projection branch.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Maintains normal variance across shortcut outputs.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Normalizes the projected skip connection.

educationalNotes.summary: Performs element-wise residual addition: F(x) + x.
educationalNotes.detailed: Adds the output of the convolutional sandwich to the skip connection. This enables gradients to flow directly backwards.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: The core mathematical mechanism that avoids vanishing gradients in deep ResNets.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Identity loops guarantee stable gradient updates.

educationalNotes.detailed: Rectifies elements to introduce non-linearity at the block output.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Prepares activated features for the next block.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Final activation step of the bottleneck block.

calculationSteps.label: Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: 1x1 bottleneck convolution to adjust dimensionality.

educationalNotes.detailed: This 1x1 filter reduces channels to 512 prior to the expensive 3x3 convolution, saving parameters.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Controls computation scale in deep bottleneck blocks.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: 1x1 convolutions act as spatial-preserving channel projection blocks.

educationalNotes.summary: Averages spatial dimensions to form a global feature vector.
educationalNotes.detailed: Computes the spatial average of each of the 2,048 feature channels. Avoids parameter bloat associated with flattening.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: GAP acts as a global regularizer and reduces downstream parameters by 98%.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Replaces heavy fully connected layers with zero parameters.

calculationSteps.label: Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: Maps the 2048 pooled features to 1000 ImageNet classifications.

calculationSteps.label: Biases
calculationSteps.explanation: One learnable bias parameter per output category.

educationalNotes.summary: Computes probabilities for the 1,000 target classes.
educationalNotes.detailed: Uses softmax activation function to compute class probabilities.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Converts numerical logits into values that sum to 1.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Classification decision layer.
